Placements.generated_by: Adding the figures of the 9th and 10th Houses (the First and Second Nieces).
meaning: Past vector: how the querent arrived at the question
Inner stance—courage, denial, fatigue, or appetite for change
Root fuel: habit, debt, loyalty, or old grief still accruing interest
Placements.utility_in_reading: Turn to the Right Witness before you grant full authority to the Judge: it sorts motive from melodrama and history from hype. It is the querent-side lane—what they confess aloud and what the chart confesses on their behalf.
Placements.essence: The dexter witness is upstream weather—momentum, bias, and unfinished business tinting the present. It seldom contradicts the First; more often it thickens the portrait until temperament reads honestly.
read_when: Career turns where years of burnout precede the résumé question
Relationship charts where an old attachment still sets the thermostat
Money crises where spending rhythms antedate the emergency
pitfalls: Reading it as prophecy of the future rather than soil in which the future grows
Under-weighting agreement with the First—when they align, lean into candor
Overwriting the figure with the story the querent prefers
examples: Founder admits they already chose to sell—Via in the Right Witness names motion decided before the casting
Student asks about an exam while Carcer names schedules and obligation—the Witness speaks study debt, not drama

Placements.generated_by: Adding the figures of the 11th and 12th Houses (the Third and Fourth Nieces).
meaning: Incoming vector: what presses against the querent now
Environmental tone—market, kin network, boss, or literal weather
Near-future hinge: offers, deadlines, rivals advancing pieces
Placements.utility_in_reading: Balance Left Witness against Right: the tension between yesterday’s momentum and tomorrow’s lever is often the whole drama. Think of it as the board’s reply to the square where the querent stands.
Placements.essence: The sinister witness names outer leverage—partners, regulators, lovers, cycles—that do not originate in the querent’s skull yet still bend outcomes.
read_when: A hiring freeze lands on an offer already drafted
A partner’s visa timeline collides with wedding plans
A landlord sells the building—tenant’s chart wants environmental facts
pitfalls: Defaulting to secret enemy; sometimes the Left Witness is timely aid
Forgetting mobile figures turn faster than stable ones
Mistaking long-tail Sentence for this nearer hinge
examples: Left Witness Acquisitio with Right Witness Amissio—resources arrive as pride or liquidity drains; trace which witness leads the exchange
Populus left with Puer right—crowd noise encouraging a rash move; counsel pacing before commitment

Placements.generated_by: Adding the figures of the Right Witness (13) and Left Witness (14).
meaning: Verdict plane—yes, no, wait, or mixed with teeth
Synthesis of both witnesses through elemental addition
Integrity check: paired dots must read even; if odd, recount the Mothers
Placements.utility_in_reading: The Judge compresses the witnesses into one hue once vectors have had their say. Geomantic arithmetic is prior art—confirm the math, then narrate the mercy or severity with proportion.
Placements.essence: Judges crystallize the quarrel between witnesses into a single quality—benefic, malefic, mixed, or diffuse. They headline the chart; houses and court-mates supply the fine print.
read_when: Binary business go or no-go after context is named
Medical layouts once specialists and sites are identified
Legal questions where counsel is already engaged
pitfalls: Discarding a Judge because the emotional answer disagrees
Skipping even-dot validation on the paired lines
Isolating the Judge from the rest of the court
examples: Judge Fortuna Major with Sentence Tristitia—success lands with a quieter cost; name the sleepless clause
Judge Populus—outcome tracks allies and rumor; glance toward the Eleventh before closing

Placements.generated_by: Adding the figure of the Judge (15) to the figure of the First House (1 - The First Mother).
meaning: Aftermath: how the verdict settles into the querent’s life
Coping arc—relief, regret, or hunger for the next crisis
Slow variables: leases, visas, therapy cadence, vesting cliffs
Placements.utility_in_reading: Sentence narrates the second wave—feelings, cash flow, and body six months past the headline. It keeps readings from stopping at champagne and rescues proportion when optimism runs ahead of structure.
Placements.essence: The Reconciler is epilogue: whether promotion nourishes partnership, whether settlement restores sleep, whether grant terms mature into golden handcuffs or genuine freedom.
read_when: Merger charts asking what culture feels like after the ink dries
Breakups where life after the lease matters as much as the split
Litigation ends leaving tax and stamina bills to pay
pitfalls: Granting Sentence the same constitutional weight as Judge—related voice, different tempo
Missing when Sentence repeats Judge—then the outcome tends to stick
Forcing catastrophe when repetition simply underscores caution
examples: Sentence Carcer after Judge Laetitia—celebration followed by structure that limits spontaneity
Caput following Cauda—new role or resource after a closing chapter
